extraction.one_line_summary: GILBERT attacked with KWIATKOWSKI 5.7km from finish, KWIATKOWSKI opened decisive gap inside final 400m but GILBERT timed final kick perfectly to win.
tactical_patterns: WELLENS attacked 32km out with no followers despite National Champion jersey, suggesting rivals content to mark later.
KWIATKOWSKI opened massive gap at 350m but blew up final 100m; early sprint commitment failed against GILBERT's late timing.
Quality selection formed on late climb with VALVERDE and KWIATKOWSKI strongest, VAN AVERMAET immediately distanced despite reputation.

rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: fourth Amstel win confirms unmatched tactical patience and finishing speed on Cauberg-type finishes
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: superior power pre-200m but poor sprint timing cost certain victory; needs discipline adjustments for uphill finishes
rider_signals.ALBASINI Michael: positive: third place despite missing key wheel shows strong late-race climbing reserves
rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: positive: followed decisive GILBERT-KWIATKOWSKI move showing unexpected uphill sprint capacity for Dimension Data
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: dropped when KWIATKOWSKI accelerated despite looking comfortable earlier; form questionable for hillier classics
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: immediately distanced on final selection climb; appears ill-suited to sustained steep gradients versus explosive cobbles
rider_signals.KREUZIGER Roman: negative: crashed late with limited 2017 racing for new team; durability concerns
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: solo attack 32km out in home region shows confidence but rivals refused to engage
